text: Debugging is a crucial skill for any programmer. When your code doesn't work as expected, the first step is identifying the bug. Look for error messages or unexpected outputs. A systematic approach is best: start by reproducing the problem, then isolate the part of the code that might be causing it. Break the problem into smaller pieces. Use debugging tools like breakpoints in your code editor to pause execution and inspect variables. Logging is also very helpful. By adding print statements or using a logger, you can see what your program is doing at different stages. Finally, keep a clear record of your testing and results.
